traits.name: core:chemosynthesis
traits.description: Building food from raw chemical energy, no sunlight required. Like the deep-sea vent microbes that oxidize sulfur or methane — the most ancient way to make a living, thriving where light never reaches.

traits.name: core:anoxygenic_phototrophy
traits.description: Harvesting light for energy without splitting water or releasing oxygen, as purple and green sulfur bacteria do. An early rung on the ladder from pure chemistry to sunlight-powered life.

traits.name: core:oxygenic_photosynthesis
traits.description: Splitting water with sunlight to make food, breathing out oxygen as waste. The cyanobacterial breakthrough that painted the sky blue and came to feed nearly every food web since.

traits.name: core:heterotrophy
traits.description: Making a living by eating others instead of making your own food. The founding strategy of the animal kingdom — consume, don't produce.

traits.name: core:absorptive_decomposition
traits.description: Feeding by dissolving dead matter with external enzymes and soaking up the nutrients. The fungal way of life — nature's great recycler, unlocking carbon the rest of the web cannot reach.

traits.name: core:mixotrophy
traits.description: Hedging bets by both making food and eating it, like the algae and protists that photosynthesize when light is good and hunt when it isn't. Flexible fuel for an uncertain world.

traits.name: core:unicellular
traits.description: Life as a single self-contained cell. The original body plan — everything one microbe needs to feed, move, and divide packed inside a single membrane.

traits.name: core:eukaryote
traits.description: The complex cell with a nucleus and internal organelles, born of an ancient merger between microbes. The gateway through which all large, intricate life would eventually pass.

traits.name: core:colonial
traits.description: Many cells living together as a loose cooperative, yet each still able to survive alone. A halfway house on the road from single cells to a true unified body.

traits.name: core:multicellular
traits.description: A single body built from many committed, cooperating cells that can no longer live apart. The leap that made trees, animals, and mushrooms possible.

traits.name: core:differentiated_tissue
traits.description: Cells specializing into distinct tissues — muscle, skin, gut, and more. Division of labor within one body, the foundation on which every complex organ is built.

traits.name: core:cellulose_wall
traits.description: Rigid walls of cellulose that let a body stand tall against gravity. The structural scaffolding of plants — stems, leaves, and towering forests all rest on it.

traits.name: core:chitin
traits.description: A tough yet flexible polymer used for walls and armor. It weaves the threads of fungi and the crunchy exoskeletons of insects and crustaceans alike.

traits.name: core:mineral_endoskeleton
traits.description: An internal frame of mineralized bone or cartilage that grows with the body and anchors muscle. The inner scaffold that lets vertebrates reach great size and power.

traits.name: core:mineral_exoskeleton
traits.description: A hard mineral shell worn on the outside for armor and support. The arthropod and mollusc solution — strong protection, but it must be shed and regrown to allow growth.

traits.name: core:hydrostatic
traits.description: A skeleton made of pressurized fluid held in shape by surrounding muscle. How worms and jellyfish keep their form and move — soft-bodied, yet surprisingly strong.

traits.name: core:silica_frustule
traits.description: An intricate glass shell of silica encasing a single cell. The exquisite, geometric armor of diatoms drifting through the sunlit plankton.

traits.name: core:radial
traits.description: A body arranged around a central axis with no left or right, like a jellyfish or sea anemone. Efficient for sessile or drifting life that meets the world from every side at once.

traits.name: core:bilateral
traits.description: A body with mirror-image left and right sides, a front and a back. By concentrating senses at the head, it primes an animal for directed, purposeful forward movement.

traits.name: core:asymmetric
traits.description: A body with no plane of symmetry at all, like a sponge. Order is optional when you simply filter the water and never need to travel anywhere in particular.

traits.name: core:segmented
traits.description: A body built from repeated modular units, like a worm or a centipede. Repetition lets segments specialize and enables flexible, finely coordinated movement.

traits.name: core:sessile
traits.description: Rooted in one place for life, letting food and mates come to you. The unhurried strategy of corals, barnacles, and trees — stay put and reach out.

traits.name: core:ciliary
traits.description: Swimming or feeding by beating rows of tiny hair-like cilia. How single cells and larvae glide through water, one coordinated stroke at a time.

traits.name: core:muscular_crawl
traits.description: Moving by rhythmic waves of muscle pressing against the ground. The slow but certain locomotion of worms, slugs, and snails.

traits.name: core:limbed_walk
traits.description: Getting about on jointed limbs that lift the body clear of the ground. The breakthrough that let animals stride, climb, and run across the land.

traits.name: core:swim
traits.description: Actively driving the body through water with fins, tails, or undulations. The fast, fluid mastery of open water, from minnow to marlin.

traits.name: core:jet
traits.description: Rocketing along by forcefully squeezing out a jet of water. The squid-and-jellyfish trick — a sudden burst of speed from a single contraction.

traits.name: core:powered_flight
traits.description: True flapping flight under a body's own power. Conquered again and again by insects, pterosaurs, birds, and bats — the moment the open sky became home.

traits.name: core:poikilotherm
traits.description: Body temperature drifts with the surroundings rather than being held steady. Cheap to run but reliant on outside warmth, like a reptile basking to start its day.

traits.name: core:homeotherm
traits.description: Burning fuel to hold a constant inner warmth. Costly but liberating — it lets a body stay active through cold nights, harsh winters, and polar extremes.

traits.name: core:nerve_net
traits.description: A diffuse mesh of nerve cells with no central brain, as in a jellyfish. The very first nervous system — able to sense and react everywhere at once.

traits.name: core:ganglia
traits.description: Clusters of nerve cells forming local processing hubs. A first step toward centralization, and the origin of coordinated, patterned behavior.

traits.name: core:centralized_brain
traits.description: Nervous tissue gathered into a true brain that commands the whole body. The seat of memory, decision, and steadily more elaborate behavior.

traits.name: core:complex_cognition
traits.description: Flexible problem-solving, learning, and even foresight. The rich inner life of crows, octopuses, and apes — behavior that reaches well beyond raw instinct.

traits.name: core:sapience
traits.description: Self-aware, abstract intelligence that wields language and tools. The rare summit of the nervous line — a mind able to ponder its own place in the world.

traits.name: core:chemoreception
traits.description: Sensing the chemistry of the world — the roots of taste and smell. The oldest of the senses, guiding life toward food and away from danger by molecules alone.

traits.name: core:photoreception
traits.description: Detecting light and dark, even without a true eye. Telling day from night and shadow from sun — the raw beginning of vision.

traits.name: core:image_eye
traits.description: A true focusing eye that forms sharp images of the world. From trilobites to eagles, the sense that has driven predator, prey, and courtship alike.

traits.name: core:electroreception
traits.description: Feeling the faint electric fields cast by other living bodies. How sharks and platypuses hunt in murky water and mud where eyes are useless.

traits.name: core:echolocation
traits.description: Building a picture of the surroundings from the echoes of one's own calls. The biological sonar of bats and dolphins, seeing clearly with sound in total darkness.

traits.name: core:magnetoreception
traits.description: Sensing Earth's magnetic field as an inner compass. How migrating birds and sea turtles navigate across thousands of miles without ever losing their way.

traits.name: core:binary_fission
traits.description: Reproducing by simply splitting in two. The swift, clone-making multiplication of microbes — no partner and no courtship required.

traits.name: core:spores
traits.description: Casting out tough, tiny spores that scatter far on wind or water and wait out hard times. The far-flung dispersal strategy of fungi, ferns, and mosses.

traits.name: core:seed_analog
traits.description: Packaging an embryo together with a food store inside a protective coat. The seed is a plant's bet on the future — dormant until conditions are finally right to sprout.

traits.name: core:external_eggs
traits.description: Laying eggs to develop outside the body. Many, cheap, and left to face the world on their own — the ancient bargain of fish, frogs, and reptiles.

traits.name: core:internal_gestation
traits.description: Carrying developing young inside the body until they are ready to be born. Fewer offspring, but each far better sheltered and nourished — the mammalian wager.

traits.name: core:naked
traits.description: A bare, unadorned body surface with no special covering. Simple and cheap, trading protection for breathability, agility, or heat loss.

traits.name: core:cuticle
traits.description: A waxy outer layer that seals moisture inside. The insect's and plant's shield against drying out — a key that helped unlock life on dry land.

traits.name: core:scale
traits.description: Overlapping plates of tough tissue shielding the skin. The flexible armor of fish and reptiles, guarding against abrasion, teeth, and the elements.

traits.name: core:feather
traits.description: Lightweight branching structures serving insulation, display, and flight. The evolutionary marvel that ultimately carried dinosaurs up into the sky.

traits.name: core:fur
traits.description: A dense coat of hair that traps warmth against the skin. The mammalian blanket that made survival through cold nights and frozen winters possible.

traits.name: core:bark
traits.description: A protective outer rind of dead tissue wrapping a woody body. Armor against fire, drought, insects, and time for the longest-lived of plants.

traits.name: core:shell
traits.description: A hard mineral case enclosing the whole body. The retreat of snails, clams, and turtles — a portable fortress carried everywhere it goes.

traits.name: core:diet_generalist
traits.description: Eating a broad menu of whatever happens to be available. The opportunist's edge when the world turns unpredictable, like a raccoon or a rat.

traits.name: core:folivore
traits.description: Living on leaves — tough and abundant, but poor in energy. It demands a long gut and endless patience, like a sloth or a colobus monkey.

traits.name: core:granivore
traits.description: Specializing in energy-rich seeds and grains. The finch's and rodent's harvest — concentrated fuel well worth cracking a hard husk to reach.

traits.name: core:hypercarnivore
traits.description: A diet of almost pure meat, demanding speed, stealth, and weapons. The apex hunter's calling, like a big cat or a wolf.

traits.name: core:filter_feeder
traits.description: Straining countless small morsels from the surrounding water. From clams to blue whales, gathering a full meal one tiny particle at a time.

traits.name: core:detritivore
traits.description: Eating dead and decaying matter, tidying the world's leftovers. The essential work of earthworms and woodlice, returning nutrients to the soil.

traits.name: core:parasite
traits.description: Living on or inside a host, taking sustenance without killing it outright. A stealthy, low-cost living — from tapeworms to ticks — always at another's expense.

traits.name: core:solitary
traits.description: Living and hunting alone, seeking others only to mate. Independence chosen over cooperation, like a leopard or an octopus.

traits.name: core:herd
traits.description: Gathering into large groups for safety in numbers. Many eyes and shared vigilance, like wildebeest on the plain or starlings wheeling as one.

traits.name: core:eusocial
traits.description: A deeply cooperative society where sterile workers serve a single reproductive queen. The colony-as-one-organism of ants, bees, and termites.

traits.name: core:pair_bond
traits.description: Forming a lasting bond between mates to raise young together. Shared parenting, as in many birds and some mammals — two invested in the next generation.

traits.name: core:size_micro
traits.description: A microscopic body, life at its very smallest. Quick to reproduce and found everywhere, but forever at the mercy of surface tension and diffusion.

traits.name: core:size_small
traits.description: A small body on the scale of an insect or a mouse. Nimble, easily hidden, and cheap to build — though never far from being someone's meal.

traits.name: core:size_medium
traits.description: A mid-sized body from dog to deer scale. The versatile middle ground, balancing reach, speed, and appetite against one another.

traits.name: core:size_large
traits.description: A large body that deters predators and dominates rivals. Powerful, but hungry — it needs plentiful food and room to roam, like a bear or an ox.

traits.name: core:size_mega
traits.description: A colossal body on the grandest scale life allows. Nearly untouchable, but ravenous and slow to breed — the realm of elephants and sauropods.

traits.name: core:cryptic_coloration
traits.description: Colors and patterns that melt into the background. Camouflage that serves the hunted and the hunter alike, from a moth on bark to a leopard in dappled shade.

traits.name: core:display_coloration
traits.description: Bold, conspicuous colors that shout rather than hide. Made for courtship or warning — the peacock's fanned tail and the poison frog's vivid flare.

traits.name: core:melanin_pigment
traits.description: Cheap, tough brown-black pigment that soaks up light and shrugs off UV, from squid ink to a raven's feather. The oldest and most versatile coloring life ever stumbled onto.

traits.name: core:carotenoid_pigment
traits.description: Reds, oranges, and yellows borrowed from food and flaunted in the skin — a flamingo's pink, a goldfinch's gold. Because you cannot fake them, they honestly advertise a good diet.

traits.name: core:pterin_ommochrome_pigment
traits.description: Bright pigments an animal builds for itself, painting butterfly wings and the fireworks in a fish's fin without waiting on its dinner.

traits.name: core:structural_thin_film
traits.description: Color made of pure geometry: stacked nanolayers that split light into shifting metallic sheens, as on a beetle's back or a soap bubble. No pigment required — just light interfering with itself.

traits.name: core:photonic_crystal_scales
traits.description: Three-dimensional nanolattices grown inside scales that trap and reflect single wavelengths with jewel-like purity, like the opal-blue of a Morpho or the impossible green of certain weevils.

traits.name: core:diffraction_grating_skin
traits.description: Rows of nanoscale ridges etched into the cuticle that fan white light into a full rainbow, a natural CD-surface worn as skin — seen in some marine worms and the sheen of a bird-of-paradise feather.

traits.name: core:iridescence_directional
traits.description: Structural color engineered to blaze one hue head-on and vanish from the side, so a courtship flash can be aimed like a spotlight and hidden from predators watching off-axis.

traits.name: core:countershading
traits.description: Dark above, pale below — a painter's trick that cancels the shadow of a rounded body and flattens it into invisibility. Worn by sharks, gazelles, and caterpillars hanging upside down alike.

traits.name: core:disruptive_patterning
traits.description: Bold, clashing blotches that shatter the body's outline so the eye cannot assemble a whole animal — the logic behind a giraffe's patchwork and a warship's dazzle paint.

traits.name: core:transparency_glass_body
traits.description: Tissues stripped of pigment and matched in refraction until the whole animal all but disappears in open water, like a glass octopus or a larval eel you can read newsprint through.

traits.name: core:silvered_mirror_skin
traits.description: Stacks of guanine crystals turning the flank into a vertical mirror that reflects the open ocean back at any onlooker — the shimmer of a herring or a hatchetfish, erasing itself with reflected water.

traits.name: core:leucophore_scatter
traits.description: Cells packed with tiny reflectors that bounce back every wavelength at once, printing brilliant matte whites and serving as the blank canvas beneath an octopus's shifting colors.

traits.name: core:chromatophore_control
traits.description: Pigment sacs slung with muscle so the animal can expand or shrink each dot of color at will, painting waves of pattern across its skin in seconds — the cephalopod and chameleon superpower.

traits.name: core:rapid_adaptive_camouflage
traits.description: A skin wired to the eyes that reads the surroundings and repaints itself to match in under a second, texture and all — the cuttlefish vanishing act, achieved even by animals that are themselves colorblind.

traits.name: core:electrochromic_skin
traits.description: Color driven directly by voltage across the skin, pigments and reflectors flipping state as nerves fire — a faster, all-electric route to changing color than muscle-hauled chromatophores.

traits.name: core:liquid_crystal_skin
traits.description: A dermis layered with liquid crystals whose spacing the animal can stretch and squeeze, sweeping its structural color smoothly across the spectrum like a living mood ring under conscious control.

traits.name: core:aposematic_warning
traits.description: Screaming reds, yellows, and blacks that advertise poison or sting, teaching predators once and forever to keep away — the honest billboard of a poison frog or a wasp.

traits.name: core:batesian_mimicry_color
traits.description: A harmless animal cloaking itself in the warning colors of a dangerous one, cashing in on a lesson it never had to teach — the palatable hoverfly wearing the wasp's stripes.

traits.name: core:mullerian_convergence
traits.description: Several genuinely dangerous species converging on one shared warning livery, so every predator's hard-learned lesson protects them all — the ringed reds and blacks worn across unrelated toxic species.

traits.name: core:deimatic_display
traits.description: A sudden flash of eyespots, gaping color, or spread frills meant to freeze a predator for the half-second needed to flee — the octopus flushing dark and rearing, the mantis throwing open painted wings.

traits.name: core:eyespot_ocelli
traits.description: Concentric rings that ape the staring eyes of a much larger animal, turning a moth's wing into the face of an owl and buying a startled moment's mercy.

traits.name: core:flash_coloration
traits.description: Vivid patches kept hidden until the animal bolts, then snapped shut again on landing so the pursuer chases a bright signal that abruptly no longer exists — the sudden blue of a fleeing grasshopper's wings.

traits.name: core:false_head_deflection
traits.description: A decoy head painted at the wrong end — false eyes and waving tail-filaments — so a strike lands on something expendable and the real head slips away, as hairstreak butterflies do.

traits.name: core:motion_dazzle
traits.description: High-contrast stripes and zigzags that scramble a predator's read on speed and heading the instant the animal moves — the reason a herd of zebra dissolves into a confusing flicker.

traits.name: core:turing_pattern_skin
traits.description: Patterns that paint themselves as competing pigment signals diffuse and react across the growing skin, no blueprint required — the mathematics behind a leopard's rosettes and an angelfish's stripes.

traits.name: core:sexual_dichromatism
traits.description: The sexes painted as if different species — one drab for hiding on the nest, the other ablaze for courtship, the mallard drake beside his mottled hen.

traits.name: core:nuptial_flush
traits.description: Colors that ignite only in the mating season and fade back to grey afterward, sparing the cost and the risk of being gaudy year-round — the salmon turning crimson, the anole flaring its throat-fan.

traits.name: core:ontogenetic_color_shift
traits.description: A wholesale repainting between life stages — the young wearing colors that hide or beg while adults wear those that court, from the spotted fawn to the reef fish reborn in new livery.

traits.name: core:uv_reflective_signal
traits.description: Markings that blaze in ultraviolet, invisible to us but shouting to eyes that see it — secret courtship badges on a bird's breast or a butterfly's wing, and nectar runways painted on flowers.

traits.name: core:uv_absorbing_markings
traits.description: Patches that swallow ultraviolet to carve high-contrast patterns against a UV-bright sky or petal, legible only to the right eyes — a private channel written in darkness.

traits.name: core:biofluorescence
traits.description: Molecules that drink in high-energy light and re-emit it as an eerie glow of another color, greening a coral or reddening a shark under blue water. Not making light — laundering it into a new hue.

traits.name: core:phosphorescent_afterglow
traits.description: Skin that hoards daylight and bleeds it back for hours after dark, an animal quietly aglow long after the sun is gone — rare on Earth, but a plausible trick for life beneath a fitful star.

traits.name: core:polarization_signal
traits.description: Messages written not in color but in the twist of light's polarization — a private channel invisible to most eyes, flashed between mantis shrimp and cuttlefish that alone can read it.

traits.name: core:polarized_camouflage
traits.description: Skin engineered to scramble the polarization signature that gives a body away in open water, hiding from the very predators — squid, crustaceans — that hunt by reading polarized light.

traits.name: core:bioluminescence
traits.description: Cold living light, conjured by an enzyme burning a fuel molecule, glowing without heat where no sunlight reaches — the firefly's lantern, the deep sea's endless private constellations.

traits.name: core:multicolor_bioluminescence
traits.description: More than one living light, tuned to different colors for different jobs — a red beam invisible to prey for hunting, a green flash for signaling, as the dragonfish and railroad worm wield at once.

traits.name: core:counterillumination
traits.description: Belly lights tuned to exactly match the glow filtering down from above, erasing the tell-tale silhouette a predator below would otherwise see — the lanternfish and firefly squid vanishing into the light.

traits.name: core:bioluminescent_lure
traits.description: A dangled, glowing bait grown to draw curious prey straight to the mouth — the anglerfish's living fishing rod burning in the black.

traits.name: core:bioluminescent_alarm
traits.description: A blaze of light thrown up when seized — not to escape, but to floodlight the attacker for its own larger enemies. The dinoflagellate's spark in a breaking wave, calling down a predator's predator.

traits.name: core:luminous_decoy
traits.description: A glowing body part shed on demand, left pulsing in the dark as a false target while the animal slips away unlit — the deep-sea worm casting off luminous 'bombs.'

traits.name: core:bioluminescent_courtship_code
traits.description: Species-specific flash sequences timed to the millisecond, a blinking Morse of light by which mates find and verify one another in the dark — the firefly's dialogue of glows, each species its own rhythm.

traits.name: core:chemiluminescent_cloud
traits.description: A discharge of luminous fluid ejected into the water as a blinding, drifting cloud of light that dazzles a hunter while the prey vanishes — the vampire squid spitting light instead of ink.

traits.name: core:thermochromism
traits.description: Skin whose color slides with its temperature alone, warming from dark to pale as the day heats — a passive display needing no nerves, plausibly worn by cold-blooded life on a world of wild temperature swings.

traits.name: core:seasonal_pelage_change
traits.description: A twice-yearly repainting of the whole coat — brown for summer earth and white for winter snow — so the animal stays hidden as its world changes color. The ptarmigan, the snowshoe hare, the ermine.

traits.name: core:mood_flush_signal
traits.description: Fast waves of color that leak the animal's state — rage, fear, courtship — across its skin in real time, the octopus flushing red-brown in anger, a signal it cannot help but broadcast.

traits.name: core:hormonal_color_change
traits.description: A slower repainting steered by hormones over minutes to days, letting an animal darken to warm in cold or pale under stress without the machinery of an active display.

traits.name: core:retroreflective_eyeshine
traits.description: A mirror behind the retina that throws light straight back the way it came, setting the eyes aglow in a predator's beam — a cat's green flash, repurposed by some animals into a deliberate signal.

traits.name: core:spectral_gradient_body
traits.description: A body shaded in a smooth sweep across the whole spectrum, head to tail, so its signal reads differently depending on which end you watch — rare and costly, a living rainbow strung along one animal.

traits.name: core:disruptive_eye_mask
traits.description: A dark band drawn across the eye to hide its tell-tale round shape and the direction of a gaze — the bandit's mask worn by so many fish and birds to keep predators from finding the head.

traits.name: core:mirror_flash_signal
traits.description: Reflective panels tilted on command to catch and hurl beams of sunlight at a rival or mate, signaling with borrowed light like a heliograph — flashes visible across distances no pigment could reach.

traits.name: core:transparent_to_opaque_switch
traits.description: An animal that can turn its own glassy tissues cloudy and back — hiding as a ghost in open water yet flushing opaque to signal or to shade its organs from a sudden glare. A dial between invisibility and display.

traits.name: core:living_screen_display
traits.description: Chromatophores shrunk and multiplied into a fine grid of independently controlled pixels, letting the animal render sharp moving images on its skin — a living display screen far past the cuttlefish's coarse patterning.

traits.name: core:predator_mimic_display
traits.description: Skin that conjures the image of a bigger, fiercer animal on demand — an octopus flattening and banding itself into the shape of a venomous flatfish or sea snake, wearing a whole other creature as a costume.

traits.name: core:colony_light_show
traits.description: Thousands of glowing individuals firing in synchrony, rippling waves and pulses of light across a whole colony as one vast display — Earth's synchronous fireflies hinting at what a truly wired colony could stage.

traits.name: core:radiofrequency_glow
traits.description: Organs that broadcast and receive weak radio-frequency emissions as a signaling channel — an alien 'coloration' no eye could ever see, patterns painted in a slice of the spectrum Earth life never learned to use.

traits.name: core:magnetic_pattern_display
traits.description: Skin studded with switchable magnetic domains that spell out patterns readable only by magnetoreceptive senses — a message you could never see, only feel as a shape in the field, on a world where life reads magnetism.

traits.name: core:infrared_thermal_display
traits.description: Controlled hot and cold patches painted across the skin as a picture in heat, legible to any eye that sees infrared — a courtship or threat signal that works in total darkness and cannot be hidden in shadow.

traits.name: core:metamaterial_cloak
traits.description: A skin of engineered nanostructures that bends light around the body rather than reflecting it, approaching true invisibility — a trick Earth life never found, but not forbidden by physics to a lineage that did.

traits.name: core:polarization_holography
traits.description: Layered polarizing microstructures that cast a different image depending on the viewer's angle and the light's twist, a living hologram flashing several hidden messages at once to eyes that can read polarization.

traits.name: core:sonoluminescent_flash
traits.description: A snap of collapsing bubbles that momentarily flares into light — an animal that shouts and glows in the same violent instant. Earth's pistol shrimp brush the edge of this; an alien lineage might build a whole signal from it.

traits.name: core:mot_flagellar_corkscrew
traits.description: A whip-like tail spun by a true molecular rotary motor, driving the cell forward in a corkscrew swim, as E. coli and countless microbes do. Earth's one genuine free-spinning wheel, hidden away at the scale of a single cell.

traits.name: core:mot_amoeboid_crawl
traits.description: Oozing forward by throwing out pseudopods and pouring the body into them, gripping and releasing the substrate like an amoeba or a hunting slime mold. The most primitive way an animal cell learns to go somewhere on purpose.

traits.name: core:mot_gliding_slime
traits.description: Sliding across a surface with no visible limbs by secreting a slick trail and ratcheting along adhesion complexes, the way myxobacteria swarm and diatoms creep. Movement without a single moving part on the outside.

traits.name: core:mot_ciliary_gliding
traits.description: Skating over a self-laid sheet of mucus on a dense carpet of beating cilia, as a flatworm glides so smoothly it seems to levitate. No legs, no waves, just a shimmering underside doing all the work.

traits.name: core:mot_metachronal_row
traits.description: Rows of paddles or comb-plates beating slightly out of phase, so a shimmering wave sweeps down the body and pushes it along, as a comb jelly's iridescent rows do. Dozens of tiny oars firing in sequence like a Mexican wave made of limbs.

traits.name: core:mot_pedal_wave
traits.description: Rippling waves of muscle travelling along a single broad foot, each ripple gripping the mucus trail and inching the animal onward, the patient signature of a snail or slug. Slow, relentless, and able to cross a razor's edge unharmed.

traits.name: core:mot_peristaltic_burrow
traits.description: Driving through soil by squeezing waves of body-segments long-and-thin then short-and-fat, anchoring with bristles and hauling the rest forward, exactly as an earthworm eats its way underground. The body becomes its own hydraulic ram.

traits.name: core:mot_inchworm_looping
traits.description: Advancing by arching the body into a loop, planting the front, then dragging the rear up to meet it, the measuring-worm gait of loopers and leeches. Locomotion reduced to a repeated bow and a repeated pull.

traits.name: core:mot_lateral_undulation
traits.description: Throwing the whole limbless body into travelling S-curves that push sideways against pebbles, stems, and ground roughness to drive forward, the classic slither of a snake through grass. Every bend is a paddle biting the world.

traits.name: core:mot_sidewinding
traits.description: Rolling the body across loose ground in a rhythm of lifted and planted arcs so only two short lengths ever touch, leaving a ladder of J-shaped tracks, the trick a sidewinder uses to cross scorching dunes. Locomotion that barely touches the earth it crosses.

traits.name: core:mot_concertina_crawl
traits.description: Bracing the body against tunnel walls in accordion folds, extending the front, then reeling the anchored rear up behind it, the way a snake climbs a smooth branch or threads a burrow. Grip, reach, gather, repeat.

traits.name: core:mot_rectilinear_crawl
traits.description: Flowing forward in a dead-straight line by walking on the belly scales themselves, ripples of skin pulling the heavy body along without a visible bend, the ghostly glide of a large python stalking prey. Stealth as a form of locomotion.

traits.name: core:mot_sand_swim
traits.description: Diving beneath dry sand and undulating through it as if it were water, the grains fluidizing around a streamlined wedge of a head, the uncanny trick of the sandfish skink. A creature that swims where there is nothing to swim in.

traits.name: core:mot_gliding_ribbon
traits.description: Flattening the body into a concave ribbon and undulating through open air to sail from tree to tree, steering the fall with continuous S-waves, the improbable flight of a paradise tree snake. A limbless animal that turns its whole body into a wing.

traits.name: core:mot_undulatory_swim
traits.description: Sending a travelling wave down a flexible body and tail to press against the water and surge ahead, the fundamental fish-and-eel stroke that anguilliform swimmers refined into pure grace. The oldest way a spine ever went fast.

traits.name: core:mot_thunniform_swim
traits.description: Holding a stiff torpedo body nearly rigid while only a crescent tail oscillates on a narrow stalk, wringing top speed and endurance from every beat, the design shared by tuna, lamnid sharks, and dolphins alike. Convergence sculpted the same fast shape three separate times.

traits.name: core:mot_porpoising
traits.description: Bursting clear of the surface in low arcs to slip through thin air instead of thick water between strokes, the energy-saving bounding of dolphins and penguins on the move. Flight briefly cheaper than swimming.

traits.name: core:mot_median_fin_undulation
traits.description: Holding the body still and rippling a long fringing fin instead, hovering, reversing, and pivoting in place with delicate control, the hovering finesse of a knifefish, cuttlefish, or ray. Precision maneuvering without ever turning around.

traits.name: core:mot_lift_based_flapping_swim
traits.description: Flying through water on wing-like flippers that generate lift on both the down- and up-stroke, the underwater soaring of penguins, sea turtles, and manta rays. Birds of the sea beating a denser sky.

traits.name: core:mot_drag_paddle_swim
traits.description: Rowing through the water with oar-shaped limbs, power stroke feathered against the return, the honest thrashing of a diving beetle or a paddling duck. Inefficient but simple, the first thing legs try when they hit water.

traits.name: core:mot_scallop_clap
traits.description: Escaping in ungainly hops by clapping two shell valves together and squirting water out the hinge, the panicked flight of a scallop from a stalking starfish. A shell that briefly becomes a jet.

traits.name: core:mot_jet_refinement
traits.description: Refining raw jet propulsion into a high-pressure pulse steered by a swivelling funnel and trimmed by fins, letting a squid rocket backward, brake, and even leap from the sea. The animal kingdom's most sophisticated rocket engine, built of muscle.

traits.name: core:mot_jet_gas_propulsion
traits.description: Venting a stored charge of metabolically generated gas in a sudden roar to kick the body across water or through the air, a living cold-gas thruster no Earth lineage ever perfected. Escape by rocketry, refueled by chemistry.

traits.name: core:mot_buoyancy_bladder
traits.description: Tuning depth with an internal gas bladder that inflates and deflates to hold the body weightless at any level without a single stroke, the swim bladder that let fish colonize the whole water column. Hovering achieved by chemistry instead of effort.

traits.name: core:mot_lipid_buoyancy
traits.description: Achieving near-weightlessness with a vast oily liver full of low-density squalene, buoyancy that cannot be crushed by the abyss, the trick of deep-sea sharks that live where a gas bladder would implode. Floating on stored fat in the crushing dark.

traits.name: core:mot_gas_float_aquatic
traits.description: Riding the boundary of sea and sky on a gas-filled float while trailing gear hangs into the water below, the wind-driven drift of a Portuguese man o' war. A creature that sails where it cannot swim.

traits.name: core:mot_living_sail
traits.description: Raising a stiff translucent sail above a small raft of a body to be pushed across the open ocean by the wind, the passive voyaging of a by-the-wind sailor. Set at an angle so whole fleets tack in opposite directions and never all wash ashore at once.

traits.name: core:mot_gasbag_aerial
traits.description: Filling a great envelope with metabolically lightened gas until the whole creature lifts off the ground and drifts among the clouds, a living balloon grazing the aerial plankton. Nothing on Earth ever floated its body into the sky this way, but an alien world might.

traits.name: core:mot_ballistic_jump
traits.description: Storing muscular energy slowly in a spring and releasing it all at once to fling the body many times its own length, the explosive escape of a flea or froghopper. Acceleration so violent that muscle alone could never manage it.

traits.name: core:mot_springtail_furcula
traits.description: Cocking a forked tail-spring latched under the belly and snapping it against the ground to somersault skyward in an instant, the panic-launch of a springtail. A tiny animal that disappears before a predator can register it moved.

traits.name: core:mot_click_escape
traits.description: Jackknifing a latched hinge in the middle of the body with an audible snap to hurl itself into a somersault, righting a beetle flipped onto its back with no legs involved. Escape by throwing yourself into the air.

traits.name: core:mot_catapult_limb
traits.description: Loading a single appendage against a mechanical latch until it releases at cavitating, water-boiling speed, the spring-loaded strike of a mantis shrimp or the escape-snap of a trap-jaw ant. The fastest movements any animal makes, powered by stored elastic rage.

traits.name: core:mot_saltation_hop
traits.description: Bounding on oversized hind limbs whose tendons store and return energy like pogo springs, so that fast travel costs almost nothing, the tireless hop of a kangaroo or jerboa. The faster it goes, the cheaper each leap becomes.

traits.name: core:mot_bipedal_run
traits.description: Balancing the whole body over two striding legs and running flat-out with a stiffened counterbalancing tail or trunk, the sprint of an ostrich or a fleeing theropod. Two limbs freed from the ground for other work entirely.

traits.name: core:mot_galloping_gait
traits.description: Cycling four legs through a rolling sequence with a full airborne suspended phase, the ground-devouring gallop of a horse or cheetah where for an instant nothing touches the earth. Top speed bought with a moment of flight between strides.

traits.name: core:mot_tripod_gait
traits.description: Walking on six legs in two sets of three so that a stable triangle always touches the ground, letting an insect scramble at full tilt without ever toppling. Speed and perfect balance at the same instant.

traits.name: core:mot_hydraulic_leg
traits.description: Straightening the legs not with muscle but with a surge of internal blood pressure, so a spider unfolds and pounces on a pulse of hydraulics, then curls up helplessly the moment its pressure fails. Legs run on plumbing, not just meat.

traits.name: core:mot_brachiation
traits.description: Swinging hand-over-hand beneath the branches in long pendulum arcs, the body a weight and the arms a rope, the effortless canopy travel of a gibbon. A gait that turns gravity into free forward momentum.

traits.name: core:mot_knuckle_walk
traits.description: Bearing weight on the backs of curled fingers so the long grasping hands stay unbruised for climbing and manipulation, the compromise gait of gorillas and chimps caught between tree and ground. Feet for the trees, fists for the earth.

traits.name: core:mot_benthic_fin_walk
traits.description: Clambering across the seafloor or a mudflat on stiffened, limb-like fins, taking deliberate hand-over-hand steps instead of swimming, the way a frogfish stalks or a mudskipper crawls onto land. Fins rediscovering what legs are for.

traits.name: core:mot_surface_tension_walk
traits.description: Standing and striding on top of the water itself, non-wetting hairs spreading the weight so each leg dimples the film without breaking through, the impossible-looking patrol of a water strider. A whole life lived on the skin of a pond.

traits.name: core:mot_basilisk_run
traits.description: Sprinting upright across open water on slapping, air-trapping feet, staying atop the surface only as long as speed and nerve hold out before sinking to swim, the famous dash of the basilisk lizard. Running on water bought by the stride.

traits.name: core:mot_planing_glide
traits.description: Taxiing across the wave-tops with a beating lower tail lobe to build speed, then spreading wing-like fins to glide hundreds of meters just above the sea, the getaway of a flying fish. Leaving the water to escape what lives in it.

traits.name: core:mot_gliding_membrane
traits.description: Spreading a stretched skin sail between the limbs to trade height for distance in long controlled swoops between trees, the patagium glide of flying squirrels, colugos, and dragon lizards. Not quite flight, but the doorway to it.

traits.name: core:mot_wing_assisted_incline
traits.description: Flapping stubby proto-wings not to fly but to press the feet harder against a slope, running up sheer surfaces on downforce, the behavior of chukar chicks that may hint at how flight began. Wings as traction before they were ever lift.

traits.name: core:mot_hovering_flight
traits.description: Beating the wings in a rapid figure-eight that generates lift on both strokes to hang motionless in the air, the jewel-like hover of a hummingbird or hoverfly sipping from a flower. Flight so controlled it becomes stillness.

traits.name: core:mot_static_soaring
traits.description: Locking the wings and circling upward inside rising columns of warm air or wind deflected off a ridge, gaining altitude for free before gliding on, the patient cross-country of vultures and storks. Miles of travel paid for by the sun heating the ground.

traits.name: core:mot_dynamic_soaring
traits.description: Harvesting energy from the wind-speed gradient just above the waves, endlessly wheeling from slow low air into fast high air to cross oceans on a single set of locked wings, the ceaseless voyaging of an albatross. Thousands of kilometers, almost never a flap.

traits.name: core:mot_ballooning_silk
traits.description: Reeling out fine strands of silk that catch the breeze and the planet's own electric field until the animal is lofted miles high and carried for hundreds of kilometers, the aerial dispersal of tiny spiders. Sailing the sky on a thread of your own making.

traits.name: core:mot_mucus_rappel
traits.description: Dropping abruptly on a self-payed line of silk to escape a predator or descend to new ground, then climbing or reeling back up, the vanishing act of a startled caterpillar or spider. An emergency exit spun on demand.

traits.name: core:mot_burrow_tunnel
traits.description: Excavating fast through packed earth with broad spade-shaped forelimbs that shear and shove the soil aside, the relentless digging of a mole or mole-cricket living its whole life underground. A body reshaped into a burrowing engine.

traits.name: core:mot_phoresy_hitchhike
traits.description: Clamping onto a larger, more mobile animal purely to be ferried to fresh habitat, riding a beetle's leg or a bird's feather for free, the dispersal strategy of pseudoscorpions and many mites. Going places by holding tight to something that already is.

traits.name: core:mot_defensive_roll
traits.description: Tucking the vulnerable body into a sealed armored ball that can then roll away downhill, the double-duty curl of a pill-bug, pangolin, or armadillo. Defense and departure in a single motion.

traits.name: core:mot_wheel_escape_roll
traits.description: Flinging the body into a spinning cartwheel or a tumbling series of backflips to flee across open ground far faster than its legs could ever run, the desperate roll of the Moroccan wheel spider and the cartwheeling stomatopod. Turning the whole animal into a runaway wheel for a few frantic seconds.

traits.name: core:mot_tumbleweed_roll
traits.description: Drying out, snapping free at the root, and rolling for miles across the land on the wind, scattering seed from every bounce, the dispersal gambit of the Russian thistle. The plant dies so the whole dead body can become a seed-sowing wheel.

traits.name: core:mot_autorotating_seed
traits.description: Shaping a seed into a single winged blade that spins like a helicopter rotor as it falls, slowing the descent so the wind can carry it far, the whirling samara of a maple. Aeronautics compressed into a fruit.

traits.name: core:mot_ballistochory
traits.description: Building tension into a drying pod until it bursts and flings its seeds meters away in a snapping explosion, the spring-loaded scattering of touch-me-nots and the squirting cucumber. A plant with no muscles that still throws its children.

traits.name: core:mot_aeroplankton_drift
traits.description: Being small and light enough to be swept up by the wind and carried through the high atmosphere for thousands of kilometers, the passive wandering of spores, tiny mites, and gossamer larvae. Travel by simply refusing to fall.

traits.name: core:mot_leg_autotomy_sprint
traits.description: Casting off a seized or pinned limb at a pre-weakened break-point and bolting free on the rest, regrowing the lost leg later, the last-ditch escape of crabs and harvestmen. Leaving a piece of yourself behind to keep the rest.

traits.name: core:mot_biological_wheel
traits.description: Growing a true free-spinning wheel-and-axle organ, a disc of living tissue that rotates without limit on a lubricated organic bearing, fed and cleared across a rotating join that Earth's plumbing could never cross above cell scale. The mythical rotor made flesh, the pinnacle of alien locomotion.

traits.name: core:mot_tank_tread
traits.description: Running a continuous belt of tissue in an endless loop over the length of the body, laying down grip-plates in front and peeling them up behind, so the creature crawls on its own caterpillar track. A conveyor of flesh no Earth animal ever built.

traits.name: core:mot_magnus_glide
traits.description: Spinning a vaned body or a rotating flap to conjure sideways lift from the airflow, steering and prolonging a glide by pure spin, a living Flettner rotor. Curving through the air on a force most creatures never touch.

traits.name: core:mot_semaphore_skate
traits.description: Sculling across the water film by releasing a wetting agent behind that yanks the body forward on a wave of shifting surface tension, the Marangoni skating of a rove beetle that has fallen onto a pond. Escaping by chemically tearing the water's own skin.

traits.name: core:mot_peristaltic_climb
traits.description: Ascending stems and tunnels by gripping alternately with front and rear suckers and squeezing waves of the body between them, the looping, telescoping climb of a leech or caterpillar going up. Every surface becomes a ladder of hold-and-heave.

traits.name: core:lithotroph_rock_eater
traits.description: This organism draws its energy directly from oxidizing raw minerals — iron, sulfur, manganese — dissolving bedrock the way Earth's chemolithotrophic bacteria etch pyrite in deep mines. Whole ecosystems can bootstrap on bare stone with no sunlight and no prey.

traits.name: core:radiotrophic_melanin
traits.description: Thick melanin skin converts incident ionizing radiation into usable chemical energy, as the black fungi thriving inside Chernobyl's reactor do. On a world bathed in a hard stellar wind, these creatures bask in gamma rays the way plants bask in sunlight.

traits.name: core:methane_cryo_solvent
traits.description: Life here runs on liquid methane and ethane instead of water, its enzymes tuned for a bath at 180 degrees below zero. Like the hypothesized swimmers of Titan's hydrocarbon seas, its membranes would instantly shatter in anything as scalding as an Earth ocean.

traits.name: core:plasma_electrophage
traits.description: A body of self-organizing charged filaments that feeds on ionized gas and electromagnetic flux, a dust-plasma pseudo-organism drifting where auroras crackle. It has no cells in any familiar sense — only sheaths of luminous, sustained plasma.

traits.name: core:thermosynthesis_gradient
traits.description: Instead of light or prey, this organism siphons energy from the temperature difference between its warm and cold ends, a living thermocouple straddling a hydrothermal seep. Where heat flows, it grows.

traits.name: core:silicon_photolithoautotroph
traits.description: Photosynthesis rebuilt on a silicon backbone, laying down glassy organosilicate tissue under starlight rather than carbon sugars. Its 'leaves' are semiconducting wafers that grow like slow, living solar panels.

traits.name: core:hydrogen_redox_breather
traits.description: Metabolism built on molecular hydrogen as the reducing fuel, thriving in the crushing reduced atmosphere of a gas-giant sky or an early anoxic world. It 'breathes' hydrogen the way Earth animals breathe oxygen.

traits.name: core:perchlorate_respiration
traits.description: This life form respires by reducing perchlorate salts, the toxic oxidizers that saturate Martian regolith, turning poison into a power source. To it, briny perchlorate mud is a banquet.

traits.name: core:magnetotrophy_field_tap
traits.description: By swinging conductive biomineral loops through a planet's magnetic field, this creature induces its own electrical current — a living dynamo that eats magnetism itself. It congregates along magnetic field lines the way moths gather at a lamp.

traits.name: core:sulfur_redox_blood
traits.description: Its circulatory fluid runs on dissolved sulfur compounds rather than iron, cycling bright yellow and green through a hydrogen-sulfide chemistry, as vent tubeworms partly do. Cut one and it bleeds something closer to molten brimstone than blood.

traits.name: core:acephalous_distributed_body
traits.description: There is no head, no center, no single self — just a roving network of interchangeable nodes that pools decisions across its whole spread mass, like a slime mold solving a maze without a brain. Cut it anywhere and both halves simply carry on.

traits.name: core:syncytial_giant_cell
traits.description: The entire organism is one enormous cell holding thousands of nuclei in a shared cytoplasm, growing meters long without ever dividing, as the seaweed Caulerpa does. It is a single bag of life pretending to be a body.

traits.name: core:modular_zooid_colony
traits.description: Many specialized individuals fuse into one super-body, each zooid a repurposed organ — some for swimming, some for feeding, some for stinging — as in a siphonophore like the Portuguese man o' war. What looks like an animal is really a committee of clones.

traits.name: core:mycelial_substrate_web
traits.description: A single fungal-analog threading kilometers of substrate, chemically wiring an entire ecosystem into one buried network, like the honey fungus that is Earth's largest organism. The forest above may just be its fruiting bodies.

traits.name: core:crystalline_accretion_body
traits.description: This life form has no soft flesh — it grows by templating new mineral lattice onto its surface, extending itself outward atom by atom like a living crystal. It heals a wound by regrowing the crystal face.

traits.name: core:opal_silica_endoskeleton
traits.description: An internal frame of biologically deposited opal — the same hydrated silica glass diatoms spin into shells — gives this creature a translucent, iridescent skeleton. Its bones catch the light and scatter it into color.

traits.name: core:magnetite_iron_skeleton
traits.description: Its skeleton is grown from magnetic iron oxide, giving it bones that respond to a compass, much as chiton teeth are capped in magnetite. Such a creature can feel a planet's field in its very frame.

traits.name: core:gasbag_pneumatophore
traits.description: Great cellular bladders fill with metabolically split hydrogen, lifting the whole organism off the ground to hang in the air like a living balloon — the sky-whale of countless imagined worlds. Its life is spent adrift on the wind.

traits.name: core:torus_ring_topology
traits.description: The body is a closed loop — a living donut with no front or back — its gut a circular tube and its nervous ring endlessly circulating signals. Nothing on Earth is built this way; it is topology as anatomy.

traits.name: core:living_sail_membrane
traits.description: A broad muscular membrane rigs itself into a sail and tacks across open plains or seas on the wind, steering by trimming its own canvas, as the by-the-wind jellyfish Velella half-does. It cannot walk; it only ever sails.

traits.name: core:tensegrity_mineral_struts
traits.description: Rigid mineral struts float in a web of tensioned fibers, none touching, so the whole skeleton is held together by balanced tension like a suspension bridge or a Kenneth Snelson sculpture. It is astonishingly light for its strength.

traits.name: core:aerogel_froth_body
traits.description: Its tissues are a solid foam of biological aerogel, mostly trapped gas, so a creature the size of a dog weighs almost nothing and drifts on the faintest breeze. Touch it and it feels like frozen smoke.

traits.name: core:helical_chiral_plan
traits.description: The entire body is wound into a single handed spiral, coiling as it grows so that left and right are meaningless — only the twist matters. Its whole life happens along a screw.

traits.name: core:fractal_branching_symmetry
traits.description: Every branch is a smaller copy of the whole, repeating the same fork at ever-finer scale like a fern frond or a Romanesco head, so the organism has no characteristic size. Zoom in and you keep meeting the same creature.

traits.name: core:spherical_radiolarian_symmetry
traits.description: A perfect ball radiating spines in every direction with no axis at all, like the glass-shelled radiolaria drifting in Earth's plankton. It has no up, no down, no forward — only outward.

traits.name: core:metamorphic_symmetry_shift
traits.description: This organism rebuilds its own body plan during life, hatching radial, maturing bilateral, and settling asymmetric — a wholesale change of symmetry no Earth lineage attempts within one generation. Each life stage is almost a different animal.

traits.name: core:rotary_wheel_drive
traits.description: A true free-spinning wheel on a living axle, nourished across a rotary bearing — the biological impossibility evolution never solved on Earth, save the bacterial flagellar motor. This creature rolls on hubs that turn forever.

traits.name: core:magnetic_levitation_glide
traits.description: By repelling its diamagnetic body against a planet's field, it hovers a hand's breadth off the ground and glides without ever touching down, the way a frog can be floated in a strong lab magnet. It leaves no tracks.

traits.name: core:reaction_gas_rocket
traits.description: It stores pressurized metabolic gas and vents it in a directed roaring jet, launching itself in true rocket flight rather than merely swimming by pulse. A startled one can fire itself clear across a valley.

traits.name: core:electrostatic_ballooning
traits.description: It spins charged filaments that ride the planet's atmospheric electric field, lofting the whole animal skyward on invisible lines of force, exactly as ballooning spiders sail on Earth's fair-weather field. It flies without wings, pushed by charge alone.

traits.name: core:tumbleweed_rolling
traits.description: The body curls into a wheel-like ball and rolls before the wind to travel and disperse, as the tumbleweed and the golden wheel spider do to flee. Locomotion becomes a controlled, tumbling fall.

traits.name: core:buoyant_aerial_drift
traits.description: Lighter than the air around it, this organism simply hangs and drifts with the currents, a living mote in a planet-wide aerial plankton, feeding as it floats. Its whole existence is passive, endless flotation.

traits.name: core:cryo_antifreeze_physiology
traits.description: Its fluids brim with antifreeze glycoproteins that keep it liquid at temperatures that would flash-freeze anything else, taken far past the Antarctic icefish that inspired it. It thrives where cold is the default state of the universe.

traits.name: core:radiative_heat_sail
traits.description: Broad black fins fan out to dump waste heat straight to space as infrared, the only way to cool in a vacuum where there is no air to carry warmth away. On a sunlit airless plain, it lives or dies by these radiators.

traits.name: core:hyperthermophile_tolerance
traits.description: Its proteins stay folded and functional at temperatures near boiling, clustering around scalding hydrothermal vents as the archaeon Pyrolobus does above 100 degrees. Heat that would cook other life is merely home.

traits.name: core:bioelectric_compute_organ
traits.description: A dedicated organ of stacked electrocytes performs actual computation in living voltage, extending the electric organ of a knifefish from a weapon into a calculator. It thinks partly in raw electric fields.

traits.name: core:superconducting_biomineral_nerve
traits.description: Nerve fibers sheathed in a room-temperature superconducting biomineral carry signals with zero resistance and near-instant speed across the whole body. Its reflexes are limited only by the speed of the current, not by lossy chemistry.

traits.name: core:em_field_distributed_cognition
traits.description: Thought happens not in a lump of brain but in a body-wide electromagnetic field, cognition smeared across the whole animal as a standing wave. There is no place you could point to and call the mind.

traits.name: core:chemical_broadcast_mind
traits.description: With no central brain, it coordinates by flooding its whole body with hormonal signals, a slow chemical parliament where every part votes by scent. Decisions arrive as a rising tide of molecules rather than a spark of thought.

traits.name: core:fiber_optic_light_nerve
traits.description: Its nerves are living light-pipes of clear biomineral that carry pulses of photons instead of ions, signaling at the speed of light down glassy fibers, as sponge spicules faintly conduct light. Its body glimmers faintly along its nerve tracts when it thinks.

traits.name: core:opal_lens_eye
traits.description: Thousands of tiny opal lenses grown from calcite or silica cover the body as a living array of perfect optics, exactly as the brittle star Ophiocoma studs its arms with crystal lenses. The whole skin can see.

traits.name: core:radio_frequency_reception
traits.description: Antenna-like organs tuned to radio wavelengths let it sense distant lightning, stellar bursts, and the crackle of an active magnetosphere far over the horizon. It perceives a sky of signals that other creatures are utterly deaf to.

traits.name: core:gravimetric_sense_organ
traits.description: A dense mineral pendulum organ reads tiny changes in the local gravity field, letting it feel buried mass, distant mountains, or the pull of a nearby moon. It navigates by the shape of gravity itself.

traits.name: core:polarized_light_vision
traits.description: Its eyes read the polarization angle of light, revealing hidden patterns, transparent prey, and the sun's position through cloud, as a mantis shrimp and a cuttlefish do. The sky it sees is streaked with invisible bands of order.

traits.name: core:seismic_substrate_sense
traits.description: Pressed against the ground, it reads the planet's tremors and footfalls through its whole body, sensing seismic waves like an elephant listening through its feet. Distant events reach it as a shudder in the rock.

traits.name: core:magnetic_map_navigation
traits.description: Beyond merely sensing the field, it carries a true internal magnetic map, reading both intensity and inclination to fix its position anywhere on the globe, as sea turtles and bogong moths navigate. It always knows exactly where it is.

traits.name: core:vacuum_panspermia_spore
traits.description: It casts hardened spores that survive raw vacuum, cosmic radiation, and absolute cold, dormant for millennia until they drift to another world, as tardigrades briefly endured open space. Its reproduction is a shot across the interplanetary dark.

traits.name: core:crystal_template_seeding
traits.description: It reproduces by shedding mineral seed-crystals that nucleate a whole new body from the surrounding substrate, growth guided by a lattice rather than a womb. Offspring precipitate out of the environment like frost from a template.

traits.name: core:aerial_spore_bloom
traits.description: It releases vast clouds of buoyant spores that ride the high winds as a living aerial plankton, seeding the whole sky at once, the way puffball fungi and fern spores drift. For a season the very air turns hazy with its young.

traits.name: core:horizontal_gene_fusion
traits.description: Individuals reproduce by merging and swapping whole genomes on contact, so lineages braid together rather than branch apart, an extreme of the gene-trading bacteria already do. Its family tree is really a tangled net.

traits.name: core:mirror_biomineral_skin
traits.description: Its hide is plated in stacked reflective biomineral that turns the body into a living mirror, bouncing away starlight and predators' gaze alike, as the silvery guanine skin of a herring hints at. It walks wearing the reflection of its world.

traits.name: core:living_crystal_carapace
traits.description: A shell of interlocking grown crystals armors the body, self-repairing by regrowing shattered facets and sharp enough to cut, far past the calcite plates of a chiton. Struck hard, it rings like glass and mends itself by morning.

traits.name: core:transparent_glass_body
traits.description: Flesh, organs, and all render nearly perfectly clear, so the creature all but vanishes against any backdrop, as the glass frog and the larval eel already manage. You can read print through its beating heart.

traits.name: core:sulfur_wax_cuticle
traits.description: A crust of sulfurous wax seals the body against acid, heat, and dehydration, letting it live in fuming volcanic vents and sulfur springs. It smells of struck matches and shrugs off brimstone.

traits.name: core:melanin_radiation_shield
traits.description: A dense melanin integument absorbs and dissipates hard radiation, letting it graze in irradiated wastelands where the ground still glows, like the extremophile fungi coating reactor walls. Its black hide is armor against the sky's poison.

traits.name: core:lithophagy_rock_grazer
traits.description: It literally eats stone, grinding bedrock in mineral gizzards to strip out edible ores and salts, the way shipworms and certain snails rasp away rock. It leaves smooth-bored tunnels through solid cliffs.

traits.name: core:metallovore_metal_feeder
traits.description: This predator eats raw metal, corroding and ingesting iron, copper, and nickel to build its own alloyed tissues, an extreme cousin of metal-oxidizing microbes. It rusts its meals before it swallows them.

traits.name: core:aerosol_cloud_grazer
traits.description: Adrift in the sky, it filters nourishment straight from atmospheric haze and cloud droplets, harvesting an airborne broth of organics, as gas-giant biospheres are imagined to do. It never lands to feed; the air itself is its pasture.

traits.name: core:osmotroph_solvent_feeder
traits.description: It absorbs dissolved organics directly across its skin from the liquid it swims in, drinking a cryogenic methane sea as nourishment without ever taking a bite. Feeding, for it, is simply soaking.

traits.name: core:electrophage_discharge_feeder
traits.description: It feeds on electricity itself, coaxing electrons straight off mineral surfaces or draining the charge of a lightning strike, as electrode-eating bacteria already do in the lab. Storms are its feeding frenzy.

traits.name: core:planetary_colony_mind
traits.description: Countless individuals wire into one continent-spanning intelligence, a single slow mind distributed across a whole biosphere, dwarfing any Earth ant supercolony. To meet one is to meet the planet thinking.

traits.name: core:tissue_fusion_supraorganism
traits.description: Separate individuals physically fuse flesh and share circulation into one merged super-being, blending bodies the way some corals and slime molds coalesce. Where a crowd gathered, a single larger creature stands.

traits.name: core:pheromone_swarm_intelligence
traits.description: The group thinks as one through drifting clouds of shared pheromone, solving problems no single member could grasp, as an ant colony or a honeybee swarm decides. Its intelligence lives in the air between bodies.

traits.name: core:size_aerial_leviathan
traits.description: Buoyed by vast gas cells, it swells to the size of a small mountain yet weighs almost nothing, a sky-borne leviathan grazing the high winds like Carl Sagan's imagined Jovian floaters. Its shadow can darken a whole valley.

traits.name: core:size_planetary_organism
traits.description: A single continuous organism sprawls across an entire world, its outermost parts unaware of one another, growth without natural bound, dwarfing any grove or fungal mat Earth has grown. It is less a creature than a living geography.

traits.name: core:opal_structural_iridescence
traits.description: Its color comes not from pigment but from a lattice of opal microspheres that split light into shifting rainbows, as opals and some weevils shimmer. It blazes with hues that change with every turn.

traits.name: core:plasma_bioluminescent_glow
traits.description: It kindles a cold ionized glow across its skin, wrapping itself in a faint living plasma halo far stranger than any firefly's chemical light. In the dark it looks like a creature sheathed in trapped aurora.

traits.name: core:thermochromic_skin
traits.description: Its hide changes color with temperature, flushing and paling as it warms and cools so its mood and its climate are written on its skin, like a thermochromic dye or a temperature-sensitive chameleon. It wears its heat as a shifting palette.

traits.name: core:chrome_metallic_sheen
traits.description: A skin of ordered metallic nanocrystals gives it a hard chrome luster, gleaming like polished steel across its whole body, far beyond the golden jewel beetle's shine. In sunlight it dazzles like spilled mercury.

traits.name: core:vantablack_lighttrap
traits.description: A forest of microscopic structures swallows nearly all incoming light, rendering the creature a hole in the world blacker than the bird-of-paradise's super-black feathers. It looks less like an animal than like an animal-shaped absence.

traits.name: core:sexual_reproduction
traits.description: Two parents shuffle their genes through meiosis and fusion, so every offspring is a fresh hand of cards. The costly, near-universal eukaryotic gamble that keeps parasites guessing and evolution moving.

traits.name: core:broadcast_spawning
traits.description: Release clouds of eggs and sperm into open water and trust the currents to make the match, as corals and sea urchins do on a single moonlit night. Fertilization left entirely to chance and numbers.

traits.name: core:internal_fertilization
traits.description: Deliver sperm directly inside the mate, sheltering the vulnerable union from a drying, dangerous world. The quiet precondition for eggs on land and live birth alike.

traits.name: core:spermatophore_transfer
traits.description: Package sperm into a neat capsule and hand it over, or leave it on the ground for her to collect, as salamanders and springtails do. Courtship at arm's length, insemination without contact.

traits.name: core:traumatic_insemination
traits.description: Skip the plumbing and stab sperm straight through the body wall into the blood, as bed bugs do. Reproduction delivered as a wound the female must survive and heal.

traits.name: core:nuptial_gift
traits.description: Win a mate by feeding her first, a captured fly wrapped in silk or a chunk of your own body, as some crickets and dance flies offer. Love bought and paid for in protein.

traits.name: core:parthenogenesis
traits.description: Females conceive without any father, cloning themselves from unfertilized eggs, as whiptail lizards and summer aphids do when times are good and males are optional.

traits.name: core:gynogenesis
traits.description: Sperm-dependent virgin birth: she needs a male's sperm merely to trigger the egg, then discards his genes entirely. The all-female Amazon molly parasitizes the mating drive of other species to reproduce.

traits.name: core:hybridogenesis
traits.description: Keep one parent's genome and discard the other's each generation, then borrow a fresh mate to repeat, a half-clonal lineage like the edible frog that carries but never truly blends its two heritages.

traits.name: core:kleptogenesis
traits.description: Steal sperm from any nearby species to spark reproduction, occasionally splicing a stranger's genes into the lineage, the genetic banditry of the all-female mole salamanders that raid the mating pools of five different males.

traits.name: core:haplodiploidy
traits.description: Fertilized eggs become daughters, unfertilized eggs become sons, letting a mother dial the sex of her brood at will. The strange arithmetic behind every beehive, where sisters are more related to each other than to their own young.

traits.name: core:paedogenesis
traits.description: Larvae that breed before they grow up, their bodies filled with daughters that eat their way out from within, the nightmare fecundity of gall midges racing through a rich food patch.

traits.name: core:sequential_hermaphroditism
traits.description: Change sex partway through life, born female and turning male with age and size, or the reverse, as clownfish and wrasses reshuffle their roles whenever the social order shifts.

traits.name: core:simultaneous_hermaphroditism
traits.description: Be fully male and female at once, so any two individuals can mate in either role, the mutual arrangement of earthworms and garden snails, sometimes settled by a duel of stabbing love-darts.

traits.name: core:chemical_sex_switch
traits.description: Whole populations flip sex on a seasonal chemical cue: a bloom of one compound in the water and the colony turns male, months later another turns it female. Reproduction timed to the planet's own chemistry.

traits.name: core:alternation_of_generations
traits.description: Life splits into two alternating bodies, a sexual form and an asexual one, each producing the other. Ferns swap between leafy fronds and thumbnail gametophytes; jellyfish between drifting medusa and rooted polyp.

traits.name: core:heterospory
traits.description: Make two sizes of spore, big ones stocked with food that become females and tiny ones that become males, the first fateful step toward the seed, taken by ancient clubmosses and quillworts.

traits.name: core:budding
traits.description: Grow a miniature copy of yourself as an outgrowth, then let it break free or stay attached, the quiet cloning of hydra and yeast that needs no mate, no egg, and no shuffling of genes at all.

traits.name: core:fragmentation_regeneration
traits.description: Break into pieces and regrow a whole body from each, so injury becomes reproduction. Cut a planarian in three and you get three worms; a single severed arm can rebuild an entire sea star.

traits.name: core:strobilation
traits.description: Stack your offspring like a pile of plates and release them one at a time. The polyp of a moon jelly segments its own body into a tower of tiny clones that peel off, one by one, and swim away as medusae.

traits.name: core:multiple_fission
traits.description: Grow many nuclei in secret, then split into a swarm of daughters all at once, as the malaria parasite bloats and bursts from a single blood cell in a synchronized flood of clones.

traits.name: core:polyembryony
traits.description: Split a single fertilized egg into many identical embryos: always-quadruplet armadillos born as natural clones, or a parasitic wasp egg that divides into two thousand larvae inside one doomed caterpillar.

traits.name: core:double_fertilization
traits.description: Two sperm for every seed: one fuses to make the embryo, the other triggers a burst of nutritive tissue to feed it. The flowering plants' signature trick, hidden behind every grain, nut, and fruit.

traits.name: core:pollination_mutualism
traits.description: Bribe animals to carry your sperm for you, trading nectar and perfume for a dusting of pollen delivered straight to another flower. The deal between blossoms and bees that reshaped both kingdoms.

traits.name: core:wind_pollination
traits.description: Cast pollen to the wind by the billions and let sheer numbers find a mate, the profligate, sneeze-inducing strategy of grasses, oaks, and pines that fills spring air with gold dust.

traits.name: core:self_pollination
traits.description: Fertilize your own flowers and guarantee seed even with no mate in sight, the reliable selfing of peas and many weeds, a kind of cloning wearing the costume of sex.

traits.name: core:apomixis
traits.description: Set seed without any fertilization at all, packing clonal embryos into ordinary-looking fruit, the way a single dandelion can fill a whole lawn with genetically identical daughters.

traits.name: core:vegetative_propagation
traits.description: Clone yourself through runners, tubers, and rooting stems until one individual becomes a meadow. Strawberries send out stolons; a single aspen grove can be one organism spanning a hillside.

traits.name: core:plant_vivipary
traits.description: Let seeds germinate while still attached to the parent, dropping fully grown seedlings like green spears into the mud below, the live-birth of mangroves racing to root before the tide takes them.

traits.name: core:ovoviviparity
traits.description: Keep the eggs inside the body until they hatch, then give birth to live young that fed only on their own yolk, the halfway house between laying and bearing used by many vipers and sharks.

traits.name: core:viviparity
traits.description: Nourish the growing young inside your body through a placenta and deliver them alive into the world. The mammalian and viperine investment that trades a thousand cheap eggs for a few well-fed lives.

traits.name: core:matrotrophy
traits.description: Feed the embryo directly from your own tissues rather than a sealed yolk, through a placenta, a uterine milk, or secretions the fetus drinks, pouring the mother bodily into the child.

traits.name: core:intrauterine_cannibalism
traits.description: The strongest embryo devours its siblings and the steady rain of unfertilized eggs, so only one or two gorged pups are ever born. The womb itself becomes an arena, as in the sand tiger shark.

traits.name: core:superfetation
traits.description: Conceive again while already pregnant, carrying litters at different stages at once so births roll out like an assembly line, a rare overlap seen in some fish, hares, and the odd human.

traits.name: core:embryonic_diapause
traits.description: Pause a fertilized embryo in suspended animation for months or years, implanting only when conditions turn kind. The kangaroo keeps a spare in reserve; the roe deer waits out the whole winter.

traits.name: core:marsupial_pouch
traits.description: Bear the young as barely-formed pink beans that crawl unaided into a pouch to finish growing fused to the teat, the marsupial compromise between laying an egg and carrying a full pregnancy.

traits.name: core:lactation
traits.description: Feed newborns on a rich secretion from the mother's own body, so the young need never forage while helpless. Mammalian milk, made even by egg-laying platypuses that sweat it from patches of skin.

traits.name: core:crop_milk
traits.description: Sweat a curd-like milk from the gut lining or skin to feed the brood though you have no mammary glands at all. Pigeons, flamingos, and even a live-bearing cockroach nurse their young this way.

traits.name: core:mouthbrooding
traits.description: Shelter eggs and fry inside your own mouth, going hungry for weeks to keep them safe, spitting them out to feed and sucking them back at the first flicker of danger, the devotion of many cichlids.

traits.name: core:gastric_brooding
traits.description: Swallow your own fertilized eggs and raise the young in your stomach, switching off your digestion entirely until you finally vomit up fully formed offspring, a trick the gastric-brooding frog took with it into extinction.

traits.name: core:skin_brooding
traits.description: Embed the fertilized eggs into the flesh of your own back, each sinking into a sealed pocket to develop and then hatch straight out through the skin, the unsettling living nursery of the Surinam toad.

traits.name: core:brood_care
traits.description: Guard, warm, clean, and feed your young instead of abandoning them to chance, the pivotal shift from a thousand orphaned eggs cast to the sea toward a handful of fiercely protected lives.

traits.name: core:cooperative_breeding
traits.description: Non-parents help raise another's young, older siblings, aunts, and unrelated helpers babysitting and provisioning at the nest, as meerkats and scrub-jays pool their labor to rear a single brood.

traits.name: core:brood_parasitism
traits.description: Lay your eggs in someone else's nest and let a duped foster parent do all the raising, the cuckoo chick that hatches early and heaves its stepsiblings, one by one, over the edge to their deaths.

traits.name: core:egg_mimicry
traits.description: Forge your eggs to match a host's in color and speckle so the fraud slips past unnoticed, a co-evolutionary arms race of ever-better counterfeits, as cuckoos race to match each warbler's clutch.

traits.name: core:semelparity
traits.description: Pour everything into one cataclysmic burst of breeding and then die: the salmon's upstream death march, the mother octopus starving over her eggs, the mayfly living a single mateless day.

traits.name: core:iteroparity
traits.description: Breed again and again across a long life, spreading the bet over many seasons rather than staking it all on one throw, the patient strategy of most birds, mammals, and long-lived trees.

traits.name: core:matriphagy
traits.description: Offer your own body as your children's first meal: the hatchlings swarm over the mother and consume her alive, leaving a hollow husk, as some crab spiders and caecilian amphibians do.

traits.name: core:diapause_dormant_eggs
